name: Turkey Loaf
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Fresh parsley or 2 tsp dry parsley
1 tbsp Fresh basil or 1 tsp dry basil
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. In a large bowl, combine the egg, bread crumbs, parsley (fresh or dried), poultry seasoning (or sage), mustard, and black pepper.
3. Add the ground turkey and chopped onion to the bowl; mix well until all ingredients are evenly distributed.
4. Press the mixture evenly into an ungreased 8 x 5 inch loaf pan.
5. Bake in the preheated oven for 35 minutes or until a meat thermometer reads 165°F.
6. Mix the sauce ingredients and spread over the top of the loaf before serving.
7. Yield: 5 servings, with each serving containing 3 oz (85 grams) and approximately 169 calories. Nutrient breakdown per serving: Protein 22 g, Fat 6 g, Carbohydrate 6 g, Fiber 0.5 g, Cholesterol 112 mg, Sodium 146 mg, Potassium 268 mg. Includes 1.5 servings of lean meat and 1 serving of vegetables.
Note: This recipe is a lower-fat version of traditional meat loaf made with ground turkey instead of beef. It can also be baked as patties by adjusting the cooking time accordingly.
